title: Anonmesh
summary: Anonmesh is an offline-first Solana wallet and messaging stack that uses BLE mesh relay and Arcium-powered confidential execution to keep payments and swaps private until connectivity is available.
description:
Anonmesh is building an off-grid cold wallet and messaging network for Solana. The product lets users create or import wallets, sign transactions offline, and relay encrypted packets over Bluetooth Low Energy mesh connections until a connected peer can settle them on Solana.

The public website and docs both explicitly tie the product to Arcium. Anonmesh describes itself as Solana in a confidential offline mode powered by Arcium, and the protocol docs document Arcium-backed confidential transaction flows, including Arcium escrow and MPC circuits for confidential execution.

bodySections.title: What_Atlas_Reviewed
bodySections.body: The official site frames Anonmesh as "Mesh First, Chain When It Matters" and positions the product as an off-grid cold wallet with private messaging, offline swaps, and local-first wallet behavior. The docs expand that into a BLE mesh transport layer, Nostr-based cryptographic messaging, offline transaction queues, and confidential Solana transaction relay.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Anonmesh is one of the clearest confirmed Arcium integrations in the directory. The docs describe confidential computation powered by Arcium for privacy-preserving Solana operations, direct RPC flows through Arcium Escrow, and Arcium MPC circuits for confidential escrow execution. Arcium's mainnet page also lists Anonmesh among verified projects coming to the network.

statusNote: Anonmesh's own website and docs explicitly describe Arcium-powered confidential Solana operations, and Arcium lists the project as a verified mainnet entry.

sources.label: Anonmesh Website

metrics.label: Execution Modes
metrics.value: Direct RPC or BLE mesh relay
